'''Jarrod Sammut''' (born 15 February 1987) is a [[Malta national rugby league team|Maltese]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}} for [[Keighley Cougars]] in [[RFL League One|League One]].

He previously played for [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and played for [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]], [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and  [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. Sammut spent time on loan from the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wildcats]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[London Broncos|London]] and [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He joined [[Workington Town|Workington]] on a permanent deal in the [[2016 RFL Championship|Kingstone Press Championship]]. He has also played for the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|second tier]]. Sammut is a [[List of dual-code rugby internationals|dual-code international]] for Malta, having represented both the [[Malta national rugby league team]] and the [[Malta national rugby union team]].

==Early life==
Sammut was born in [[Blacktown, New South Wales|Blacktown]], [[New South Wales]], Australia to parents Darren and Kim. He is of [[Indigenous Australians|Indigenous Australian]] descent through his mother, and [[Maltese people|Maltese]] descent through his paternal grandparents.<ref>{{cite web|last=Walshaw|first=Nick|date=6 June 2008|title=Panthers poster boy: Jarrod Sammut|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/panthers-poster-boy-jarrod-sammut/story-e6frexqi-1111116550728|publisher=Daily Telegraph|access-date=17 October 2016}}</ref>

He was educated at [[Patrician Brothers' College Blacktown]] and played junior rugby league for the [[Doonside Roos]].

==Playing career==
[[File:JARROD SAMMUT.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sammut playing in the NRL for the Penrith Panthers against the Parramatta Eels in 2009]]
===Penrith Panthers===
Sammut was a member of the [[Penrith Panthers]] squad that defeated [[Newcastle Knights]] in the 2006 [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] grand final. He made his NRL début for the Panthers on 4 August 2007 against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]. On 30 September 2007, Sammut captained the Panthers' side in their [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] Grand Final, scoring 1 try, 3 goals, and 1 field goal in their 19-14 win over the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Fitzgibbon|first=Liam|date=30 September 2007|title=Penrith comeback seals Jersey Flegg grand final|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/panthers-win-flegg-final/story-e6frexnr-1111114535938|publisher=Daily Telegraph|access-date=17 October 2016}}</ref>

===Crusaders RL===
In April 2010, Sammut was released from the final year of his Panthers' contract to join the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-and-a-half-year contract until the end of 2012.<ref>{{cite web|date=28 April 2010|title=Crusaders sign Malta scrum-half Jarrod Sammut|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/celtic_crusaders/8649438.stm|publisher=BBC|access-date=3 May 2010}}</ref> 
[[File:Jarrod Sammut.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Sammut playing for Crusaders RL in 2010]]
His arrival in Wales was delayed by [[2010 eruptions of Eyjafjallajökull|volcanic ash]] and a broken down plane. The delay meant that Sammut made it to Wales only one day before the team headed to [[Edinburgh]] for the [[Magic Weekend]].<ref name="Jarrod Sammut goes straight into Crusaders squad for Bradford clash">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailypost.co.uk/sport-news/crusaders-super-league/2010/04/30/jarrod-sammut-goes-straight-into-crusaders-squad-for-bradford-bulls-clash-55578-26346775/ |title=Jarrod Sammut goes straight into Crusaders squad for Bradford Bulls clash |publisher=Daily Post North Wales |date=30 April 2010 |access-date=3 May 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120314093210/http://www.dailypost.co.uk/sport-news/crusaders-super-league/2010/04/30/jarrod-sammut-goes-straight-into-crusaders-squad-for-bradford-bulls-clash-55578-26346775/ |archive-date=14 March 2012 }}</ref> Despite the late arrival and [[jet lag]], Sammut scored a try in his Crusaders debut.<ref name="Sammut off to a roaring start for Crusaders">{{cite web|url=http://www.thenational.ae/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20100502/SPORT/705019846/1173|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130114181852/http://www.thenational.ae/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20100502/SPORT/705019846/1173|url-status=dead|archive-date=14 January 2013|title=Sammut off to a roaring start for Crusaders|publisher=The National|date=1 May 2010|access-date=3 May 2010}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
Sammut joined the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2012 on a one-year contract. During the 2013 pre-season, he signed a new two-tier extension to his contract.{{citation needed|date=November 2016}}

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
After playing for the Bradford Bulls in a 2014 pre-season match, Sammut signed a two-year contract with the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on 14 February 2014, one day before the start of the Super League season.

On 25 June 2014, Sammut joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] on a dual-registration.<ref>{{cite web|date=25 June 2014|title=Wakefield's Jarrod Sammut loaned to Championship's Featherstone|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/9361179/super-league-wakefields-jarrod-sammut-loaned-to-championships-featherstone|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=17 October 2016}}</ref> He played in their match against the [[North Wales Crusaders]] (the successor of his former club), kicking 10 goals from 10 attempts.<ref>[http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/featherstone-vs-crusaders/40178 Featherstone vs Crusaders]. Retrieved 17 October 2016.</ref><ref>[http://www.featherstonerovers.net/article/45405/featherstone-rovers---nw-crusaders Featherstone Rovers 56-18 NW Crusaders] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161112204828/http://www.featherstonerovers.net/article/45405/featherstone-rovers---nw-crusaders |date=12 November 2016 }}. Retrieved 17 October 2016.</ref>

On 26 February 2015, Sammut joined the [[London Broncos]] on a dual-registration.<ref>{{cite web|date=26 February 2015|title=Broncos sign dual registration deal with Wakefield Wildcats|url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/broncos-sign-dual-registration-deal-with-wakefield-wildcats/|publisher=LondonBroncosRL.com|access-date=18 October 2016|archive-date=12 November 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161112151140/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/broncos-sign-dual-registration-deal-with-wakefield-wildcats/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Workington Town===
On 23 December 2015, Sammut signed with [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web|date=23 December 2015|title=Jarrod Sammut is a Townie|url=http://www.townrlfc.com/news/article/48128/jarrod-sammut-is-a-townie|publisher=TownRLFC.com|access-date=17 October 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=24 December 2015|title=RL ace Jarrod Sammut returns to Workington Town|url=http://www.timesandstar.co.uk/sport/town/RL-ace-Jarrod-Sammut-returns-to-Workington-Town-367e6512-ea31-422d-9936-cdf1adafbdaf-ds|website=Times and Star|access-date=17 July 2016}}</ref>
[[File:Jarrod Sammut London Broncos.jpg|thumb|upright|Sammut warming up for the London Broncos]]

===London Broncos===
Sammut signed a two-year deal with the [[London Broncos]] on 25 October 2016.<ref>{{cite web|date=25 October 2016|title=Jarrod Sammut signs for London Broncos|url=https://www.rugbyam.co.uk/articles/news/championship/jarrod-sammut-signs-for-london-broncos/|publisher=Rugby AM|access-date=12 November 2016|archive-date=12 November 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161112144943/https://www.rugbyam.co.uk/articles/news/championship/jarrod-sammut-signs-for-london-broncos/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=26 October 2016|title=Jarrod Sammut joins London Broncos|url=http://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/2016/10/26/jarrod-sammut-joins-london-broncos|publisher=SBS|access-date=12 November 2016}}</ref>
[[File:Jazz Sammut.jpg|thumb|upright|Sammut playing for the London Broncos in 2017]]

===Leigh Centurions===
On 5 December 2019 it was announced that Sammut would join the [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/56101/jarrod-sammut-joins-leigh|title=JARROD SAMMUT JOINS LEIGH|website=www.rugby-league.com|access-date=19 Dec 2020|date=5 Dec 2019}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 3 December 2021, he signed for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=RugbyLeague.com|date=3 Dec 2021|accessdate=4 Dec 2021|title=Barrow Raiders sign Jarrod Sammut|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36335/barrow-raiders-sign-jarrod-sammut}}</ref>

On 1 December 2023 he left [[Barrow Raiders]] and become a free-agent.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Dec 2023|title=Jarrod Sammut becomes a free agent as former Super League half-back departs Barrow Raiders|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jarrod-sammut-becomes-a-free-agent-as-former-super-league-half-back-departs-barrow-raiders}}</ref>

===Workington Town RLFC (rejoin)===
On 10 January 2024 he signed for [[Workington Town RLFC]] in [[RFL League 1]|League One]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Jan 2024|title=Jarrod Sammut's next destination confirmed with ex-Super League star set to play in new division for first time|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jarrod-sammuts-next-destination-confirmed-with-ex-super-league-star-set-to-play-in-new-division-for-first-time}}</ref> 

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 30 July 2024, [[Bradford Bulls]] announced that Sammut had returned to the club on a loan deal until the end of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/2649/bulls-re-sign-sammut |title=Bulls re-sign Sammut |website=Bradford Bulls |date=30 July 2024 |access-date=13 August 2024}}</ref>

===Keighley Cougars===
Released by Workington at the end of the 2024 season, Sammut joined League One Keighley Cougars on a one-year contract on 28 January 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Cougars snaffle Sammut |url=https://keighleycougars.uk/cougars-snaffle-sammut |publisher=Keighley Cougars |access-date=28 January 2025 |date=28 January 2025}}</ref> After making three appearances for the club, his contract was cancelled by mutual consent on 28 March 2025.<ref>{{cite news |title=Former Super League star Jarrod Sammut sees League 1 contract terminated |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-star-jarrod-sammut-sees-league-1-contract-terminated |access-date=28 March 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=28 March 2025 |language=en}}</ref>

==Representative career==
===Rugby League===
Sammut made his Test début for [[Malta national rugby league team|Malta]] on 8 October 2006 in their match against [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]].<ref name=malta>{{cite web|date=31 July 2007|title=Sammut in NRL debut, lifts Panthers to rare home win|url=http://www.malteserugbyleague.com/article.php?subaction=showfull&id=1185870620|publisher=Maltese Rugby League|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070824180058/http://www.malteserugbyleague.com/article.php?subaction=showfull&id=1185870620&archive=&start_from=&ucat=|archive-date=24 August 2007}}</ref> His 36-point haul against [[Norway national rugby league team|Norway]] in the [[2011 European Shield]] equaled the record for most points scored by a player in an international match, according to the [[Rugby League European Federation|RLEF]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Sammut equals point-scoring record|url=http://www.malteserugbyleague.com/news/1-latest-news/542-sammut-equals-point-scoring-record|publisher=Maltese Rugby League|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927100218/http://www.malteserugbyleague.com/news/1-latest-news/542-sammut-equals-point-scoring-record|archive-date=27 September 2011}}</ref> Sammut was named in Malta's train-on squad for their 28 October 2016 Test match against [[Thailand national rugby league team|Thailand]].<ref>{{cite web|date=6 September 2016|title=Malta name squad for Ireland & Thailand matches|url=http://www.asiapacificrl.com/2016/09/06/malta-name-squad-ireland-thailand-matches/|publisher=Asia Pacific Rugby League Confederation|access-date=17 October 2016}}</ref> The match was cancelled following the death of Thailand's King [[Bhumibol Adulyadej]].<ref>{{cite web|date=17 October 2016|title=Malta tour of Thailand cancelled|url=http://www.asiapacificrl.com/2016/10/17/malta-tour-thailand-cancelled/|publisher=Asia Pacific Rugby League Confederation|access-date=12 November 2016}}</ref>

Sammut has also represented Malta in non-Test matches. On 19 October 2007, he scored a try against the [[Royal Logistic Corps]]' rugby league team.{{citation needed|date=May 2017}} In October 2009, Sammut represented Malta in the Australian Mediterranean (Aus-Med) Shield at [[Marconi Stadium]] in Sydney, a tournament made up completely of Australian residents, such as [[Blake Austin]], [[John Skandalis]] and [[Cameron Ciraldo]].<ref>{{cite web|date=6 October 2009|title=Aus-Med Shield Tournament|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/nswcup/article.php?id=818|publisher=NSWRL.com.au|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091009024608/http://www.nswrl.com.au/nswcup/article.php?id=818|archive-date=9 October 2009}}</ref> Sammut played in both of Malta's games, against [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] and [[Portugal national rugby league team|Portugal]].

===Rugby Union===
On 25 October 2008, Sammut represented the [[Malta national rugby union team]] in their [[2011 Rugby World Cup qualifying]] match against [[Croatia national rugby union team|Croatia]]. He kicked 3 penalty goals and 1 conversion for a total of 11 points in Malta's 16-18 loss.<ref>{{cite web|last=Camilleri|first=Valhmor|date=26 October 2008|title=Last-gasp penalty foils Malta|url=http://www.timesofmalta.com/articles/view/20081026/rugby/last-gasp-penalty-foils-malta.230569|publisher=Times of Malta|access-date=17 October 2016}}</ref> Sammut was to also play against [[Sweden national rugby union team|Sweden]] on 1 November 2008, but was pulled from the game after the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] learned he was playing union, not league.<ref>{{cite web|last=Mascord|first=Steve|date=29 October 2008|title=Sammut sidelined after contract breach|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sammut-sidelined-after-contract-breach/story-e6frexqi-1111117881524|publisher=Daily Telegraph|access-date=16 October 2016}}</ref>
